description: White to play at the top right. In this full board position, KataGo does not like the traditional joseki at A! What should white do instead?
sgf:
(;SZ[19]AP[Jago:Version 5.0]GM[1]FF[1]CA[UTF-8]AW[dp][od][ec][cc][dd][ce][bd][ee][dk][qd]AB[qp][cd][jc][gc][bc][cb][dc][cc][eb][qc][qe]LB[pd:A]GN[08-10-04-xela-anglevide20](;W[rd];B[pd](;W[pc];B[pe](;W[re];B[oc];W[pb];B[qg]C[RIGHT
White can tenuki now, and play in one of the bottom corners.])(;W[qb];B[oc](;W[pb];B[re]C[Getting sealed in like this is bad news!])(;W[rc];B[pb];W[qc];B[nb]TR[od]C[From here, KataGo shows a couple of options for white trying to pull out the marked stone or start a fight nearby, and it looks complicated, but black has a clear advantage in all variations.]))(;W[rc];B[oc];W[pb];B[ob];W[qb];B[op]C[For this full board position, black's influence is worth more than white's corner profit.])(;W[pb];B[rc])(;W[oc];B[rc]))(;W[pe];B[pc](;W[qf];B[oe](;W[pf];B[nd]TR[jc][gc]C[KataGo thinks this is about 2 points worse for white than the recommended line. Black's influence works well with the marked stones. If white were strong at the top left, then this variation would be better.])(;W[re];B[pf]C[White doesn't have big enough ko threats to play this way.]))(;W[pf];B[re];W[rf];B[rc];W[qf];B[sd]C[Black's corner profit is too big.])(;W[rc];B[qf];W[pf];B[qg];W[oc];B[rb]C[White's three stones can't escape])(;W[re];B[qf](;W[rf];B[qg])(;W[rc];B[rb]C[White's four stones are in trouble!]))))(;W[pc];B[rd];W[pd];B[rb];W[qb];B[rc]C[White's shape is too heavy.])(;W[pd];B[rd]C[I told you KataGo didn't like this move! But let's play on a bit and see what happens.](;W[rc];B[rb];W[re];B[sd];W[qf];B[fp]C[In this full board position, black's ladder breaker is very effective. (I can't say I fully understand this. But it's what the machine is telling us!)])(;W[re];B[rf];W[rc];B[se];W[qb]TR[jc][gc]C[White's thickness isn't effective with the marked stones nearby.])))
